topic: Design Patterns Under Pressure for Low-Level System Design
summary: A 28-lesson deep-study course that teaches a beginner to evaluate, apply, criticize, and combine major low-level design patterns one pattern at a time, using design pressure and tradeoff analysis as the organizing lens.

modules.title: Pressure-First Foundations
modules.strata_theme: Theme 1: Patterns are pressure-responses, not catalog trivia
modules.description: Build the decision model for the whole course: patterns exist because recurring forces make simple designs too costly.
objectives: Identify and define the recurring pressures that make patterns useful
Apply a pressure-first method before selecting a pattern
Explain how pattern languages differ from catalog memorization

lessons.title: Why Patterns Exist at All
lessons.description: Why coupling, volatility, coordination, and boundary mismatch create recurring design problems that patterns answer.

lessons.title: Diagnosing Pressure Before Adding Structure
lessons.description: A practical method for converting observed design pain into a justified structural response instead of speculative architecture.

lessons.title: Pattern Languages and Design Vocabulary
lessons.description: How patterns function as a language of related design moves rather than isolated names on a checklist.

modules.title: Construction and Runtime Variation
modules.strata_theme: Theme 2: Creational patterns earn their keep when construction or runtime variation becomes a real source of coupling
modules.description: Study the major patterns that reshape object creation, configuration, and runtime extension.
objectives: Apply creational patterns when construction has become a real source of coupling
Compare dependency injection, plugins, and prototype-style variation by the pressure they relieve
Judge when creational flexibility is solving a real problem versus adding wiring overhead

lessons.title: Dependency Injection as a Construction Boundary
lessons.description: How dependency injection separates configuration from use and what construction pressures justify the indirection.

lessons.title: Plugin as a Runtime Extension Pattern
lessons.description: How plugin architectures centralize runtime variability instead of scattering conditionals and environment-specific branches.

lessons.title: Prototype and the Cost of Cloning-Based Flexibility
lessons.description: When prototype-style cloning earns its keep and when modern language features or direct construction are cleaner.

modules.title: Ambient Access and Hidden Dependencies
modules.strata_theme: Theme 3 and Theme 5 overlap around global convenience patterns and hidden coupling
modules.description: Study the convenience-oriented access patterns that save plumbing while risking invisible coupling and harder reasoning.
objectives: Compare registry, service locator, and singleton by the convenience they offer and the coupling they hide
Diagnose when ambient access is justified narrowly and when it becomes architectural debt
Evaluate dependency visibility as a first-class design concern

lessons.title: Registry: Centralized Access with Hidden Cost
lessons.description: How registries centralize lookup and why that convenience can quietly widen dependency webs.

lessons.title: Service Locator: A Legitimate Tool with Sharp Edges
lessons.description: Why service locator remains useful in some environments and why it is also one of the easiest patterns to misuse.

lessons.title: Singleton Beyond the Joke Version
lessons.description: What singleton actually solves, why it is commonly abused as ambient global access, and how to recognize both cases.

modules.title: Application and Boundary-Shaping Patterns
modules.strata_theme: Theme 3: Structural patterns are boundary-management tools whose main value is selective decoupling
modules.description: Study patterns that define application boundaries and isolate awkward cases so they do not leak across the system.
objectives: Apply service and special-case patterns at the boundaries where mismatches actually occur
Use structural indirection to localize complexity instead of spreading it
Select boundary patterns based on mismatch type rather than generic decoupling slogans

lessons.title: Service Layer as Orchestration Boundary
lessons.description: How service layers centralize application orchestration and where they stop so they do not hollow out the domain.

lessons.title: Special Case for Edge Conditions Without Branch Explosion
lessons.description: How special case objects replace repeated null and sentinel handling with local, polymorphic behavior.

lessons.title: Choosing the Right Structural Pattern for the Boundary
lessons.description: A selection workshop that matches concrete boundary problems to service layer, special case, registry, or other structural responses.

modules.title: Persistence Boundaries in Depth
modules.strata_theme: Theme 3 and Theme 6 around persistence separation and composition
modules.description: Study the persistence patterns that protect the domain from relational concerns while keeping writes and queries manageable.
objectives: Apply Data Mapper, Repository, and Unit of Work to distinct persistence pressures
Compare query abstraction, object-relational isolation, and transactional coordination as separate concerns
Diagnose when persistence patterns are clarifying the design and when they are decorative ceremony

lessons.title: Data Mapper: Keeping the Domain Ignorant of the Database
lessons.description: How Data Mapper isolates object models from relational models and what complexity justifies that extra layer.

lessons.title: Repository: Query Boundaries That Still Feel Like Collections
lessons.description: How repositories centralize query logic, preserve cleaner domain access, and sometimes drift into needless indirection.

lessons.title: Unit of Work: Transaction-Scope Coordination
lessons.description: How Unit of Work coordinates multi-object changes and writeback timing without turning every save into a local event.

modules.title: Behavioral Patterns One by One
modules.strata_theme: Theme 4: Behavioral patterns manage time, coordination, and changing roles, but they trade directness for control
modules.description: Study the flagship behavioral patterns in depth instead of collapsing them into one survey lesson.
objectives: Apply Command, Observer, and State to the specific coordination pressures they address
Explain the clarity and traceability cost introduced by each behavioral pattern
Compare behavioral pattern fit across editors, workflows, and event-driven systems

lessons.title: Command: Reified Actions, Undo, and Replay
lessons.description: How Command turns actions into first-class objects so a system can defer, queue, replay, or undo work.

lessons.title: Observer: Notifications, Fan-Out, and Hidden Flow
lessons.description: How Observer enables decoupled notification while also making control flow, failure propagation, and timing harder to see.

lessons.title: State: Making Behavioral Change Explicit
lessons.description: How State and FSM-style design make behavior changes explicit enough to reason about when condition-heavy code starts to sprawl.

modules.title: Modeling Business Complexity
modules.strata_theme: Theme 5 and Contradiction 3 around procedural simplicity versus rich domain behavior
modules.description: Study the patterns and anti-patterns that decide whether behavior should remain procedural or live inside a richer domain model.
objectives: Compare Transaction Script and Domain Model by the complexity they are meant to absorb
Identify the pressure that turns a script-shaped design into a modeling problem
Detect Anemic Domain Model as a mismatch between object form and behavioral substance

lessons.title: Transaction Script: Simple on Purpose
lessons.description: Why Transaction Script remains a legitimate pattern when workflow complexity is modest and richer modeling would add unnecessary indirection.

lessons.title: Domain Model: Behavior Where the Concepts Live
lessons.description: How Domain Model becomes valuable when business rules, relationships, and stateful interactions are dense enough to justify richer objects.

lessons.title: Anemic Domain Model and the Complexity Curve
lessons.description: How to spot a design that preserved the outer shell of object modeling while pushing the real behavior elsewhere.

modules.title: Criticism, Overuse, and Pattern Economics
modules.strata_theme: Theme 5: The central failure mode is not using patterns, but paying their indirection cost before the pressure is real
modules.description: Study the strongest anti-pattern lessons and the real debates that prevent this course from becoming dogmatic.
objectives: Detect premature indirection before it hardens into architecture
Compare dependency strategies without pretending the evidence yields a universal rule
Explain how language evolution changes pattern form without erasing the underlying problem

lessons.title: YAGNI, Premature Indirection, and Abstraction Debt
lessons.description: The main economic lesson of the research: abstractions must pay rent by solving a present cost, not a hypothetical future one.

lessons.title: Dependency Injection vs Service Locator vs Explicit Passing
lessons.description: A deep comparison of three dependency access strategies and the contextual factors that decide between them.

lessons.title: When Languages Outgrow Pattern Templates
lessons.description: Why some classic class-heavy implementations age badly while the force behind the pattern remains useful.

modules.title: Composition Studios
modules.strata_theme: Theme 6: Coherent composition comes from layering small, force-specific patterns around a problem, not from stacking abstractions indiscriminately
modules.description: Use integrated case studies to learn how patterns compose when each one owns a distinct pressure.
objectives: Compose multiple patterns by responsibility rather than by category coverage
Apply persistence, service, domain, and coordination patterns in one coherent design
Refactor a naive design into a staged pattern stack only as pressures appear

lessons.title: Composing the Enterprise Stack
lessons.description: How service layer, domain model, data mapper, repository, and unit of work fit together when each abstraction owns one pressure.

lessons.title: Composing Editor and Workflow Patterns
lessons.description: How command, observer, and state combine in interaction-heavy systems and where the source base remains thinner.

lessons.title: From Naive Design to Coherent Pattern Stack
lessons.description: A guided refactor path showing how patterns should accumulate only when a concrete pressure actually appears.

modules.title: Limits, Gaps, and Honest Uncertainty
modules.description: Close the course by making the real limits of the evidence explicit instead of pretending pattern judgment can be reduced to a formula.
objectives: Explain the main unresolved limits in the research base honestly
Identify what evidence would be needed to sharpen the remaining open questions
Use research limits to calibrate confidence rather than to discard the findings

lessons.title: What This Research Still Cannot Settle
lessons.description: The remaining limits of the research, including missing GoF excerpts, context-dependent indirection thresholds, and thinner editor case studies.

learning_plan.notes: This revised map is intentionally comprehensive. It keeps the pressure-first foundation, but now gives each major pattern or pattern cluster its own deeper lesson. A typical 45-minute session can cover one deep lesson with notes and reflection, or two shorter review-oriented lessons. The milestones mark the transitions from foundations to construction, then to persistence, then to domain modeling, and finally to full composition.
